message:
fix(mvcc): replace bare .lock() with .expect() on scheduler mutex across tests

Replace all bare `runtime.scheduler.lock()` calls with
`.lock().expect("scheduler lock not poisoned")` in test code across
ffs-block, ffs-mvcc, and mvcc_stress_suite. This makes mutex poisoning
a clearly-reported panic instead of a silent unwrap, covering ~23 call
sites in concurrent reader/writer tests, SSI write-skew scenarios, GC
stress tests, and version chain growth benchmarks.

Also modernize one format string in ffs-core test from positional
`"{:?}", data` to inline capture `"{data:?}"` style.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
